# katapult_object_storage_access_key (Resource)

Manages an access key for a Katapult object storage cluster.

Use `access_key_id`, `secret_access_key`, and `server_url` to configure an object storage client or SDK. Bucket-level permissions are managed via `read_key_ids` / `write_key_ids` on `katapult_object_storage_bucket` resources; `read_buckets` and `write_buckets` here reflect those associations.

The key is scoped to the region of the `katapult_object_storage_account` it references via `object_storage_account_id`.

~> **Note:** `secret_access_key` is only available at creation time and cannot be retrieved again — it will be empty after import. Changing `object_storage_account_id` forces a new resource.

An access key is the credential a workload uses to talk to Katapult's
object storage API. It pairs with one or more
[`katapult_object_storage_bucket`](./object_storage_bucket.md) resources to
control what the workload can read or write.

The key is scoped to a single object storage account via
`object_storage_account_id`. You must declare a
[`katapult_object_storage_account`](./object_storage_account.md) resource (or
look one up via the
[data source](../data-sources/object_storage_account.md)) before declaring
any access keys.

## Regions

Object storage is currently available in a single region, `uk-lon-1`
(London, UK). The account's region is inherited through
`object_storage_account_id`; an access key can only authenticate against
buckets that live in the same account.

## Credential Lifecycle

* `secret_access_key` is **only returned once**, at creation time. It is
  never readable again — not via the Katapult API, not on
  `terraform refresh`, and not after `terraform import`.
* The secret is stored in Terraform state. Treat the state file as
  sensitive: encrypt remote state at rest, restrict access, and avoid
  printing the value to logs or unredacted outputs.
* Imported keys will have `secret_access_key` set to an empty string. If
  you need the secret for an imported key, delete and recreate the key (or
  retrieve it from your secrets store, if you stashed it elsewhere).
* Rotating a key requires creating a new access key, updating the consumer to
  use the new credentials, then destroying the old key. Use a second
  `katapult_object_storage_access_key` block during the overlap.

## Permissions Model

Permissions can be granted at two levels:

1. **Cluster-wide** — the `all_buckets_read`, `all_objects_read`, and
   `all_objects_write` attributes on the access key grant broad permissions
   across every bucket in the region. Reserve these for trusted operator or
   backup keys.
2. **Per-bucket** — preferred for application workloads. Add the access key's
   `id` to the `read_key_ids` and/or `write_key_ids` set on a
   [`katapult_object_storage_bucket`](./object_storage_bucket.md). The
   bucket's name will then appear in `read_buckets` / `write_buckets` on this
   resource after the next refresh — these attributes are read-only and
   reflect the bucket side of the relationship.

## Using the Credentials

The three attributes you typically pass to an object storage client are:

* `access_key_id` — the access key ID.
* `secret_access_key` — the secret. **Sensitive** — mark any output
  surfacing this value as `sensitive = true`.
* `server_url` — the regional object storage endpoint. Pass this as the
  endpoint URL for your client, and prefer path-style addressing if your
  client supports both.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Required entrypoint: one account per (organization, region).
resource "katapult_object_storage_account" "main" {
  region = "uk-lon-1"
}

# Minimal — key with no global permissions
resource "katapult_object_storage_access_key" "app" {
  name                      = "app-server"
  object_storage_account_id = katapult_object_storage_account.main.id
}

# Key with cluster-wide read/write access
resource "katapult_object_storage_access_key" "admin" {
  name                      = "ci-admin"
  object_storage_account_id = katapult_object_storage_account.main.id

  all_buckets_read  = true
  all_objects_read  = true
  all_objects_write = true
}

# Use the credentials to configure an object storage client
resource "katapult_object_storage_access_key" "backup" {
  name                      = "backup-agent"
  object_storage_account_id = katapult_object_storage_account.main.id
}

resource "katapult_object_storage_bucket" "backups" {
  name                      = "my-org-backups"
  object_storage_account_id = katapult_object_storage_account.main.id
  read_key_ids              = [katapult_object_storage_access_key.backup.id]
  write_key_ids             = [katapult_object_storage_access_key.backup.id]
}

output "backup_access_key_id" {
  value = katapult_object_storage_access_key.backup.access_key_id
}

output "backup_secret_access_key" {
  value     = katapult_object_storage_access_key.backup.secret_access_key
  sensitive = true
}

output "backup_server_url" {
  value = katapult_object_storage_access_key.backup.server_url
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Human-readable name for the access key.
- `object_storage_account_id` (String) ID of the `katapult_object_storage_account` resource this key is scoped to. The account ID is the region permalink, e.g. `uk-lon-1`. Changing this forces a new resource.

### Optional

- `all_buckets_read` (Boolean) Allow this key to list all buckets in the cluster. Defaults to `false`.
- `all_objects_read` (Boolean) Allow this key to read objects across all buckets in the cluster. Defaults to `false`.
- `all_objects_write` (Boolean) Allow this key to write objects across all buckets in the cluster. Defaults to `false`.

### Read-Only

- `access_key_id` (String) Access key ID for authenticating object storage clients.
- `id` (String) Internal Katapult ID of the access key.
- `read_buckets` (Set of String) Bucket names this key can read from. Populated via a bucket's `read_key_ids`.
- `secret_access_key` (String, Sensitive) Secret access key. Available only at creation; not retrievable via the API. Empty after import.
- `server_url` (String) Endpoint URL for configuring object storage clients.
- `write_buckets` (Set of String) Bucket names this key can write to. Populated via a bucket's `write_key_ids`.

## Import

Access keys are imported by their Katapult ID (the `objkey_…` value
visible in the Katapult dashboard or returned by the API).

Import is supported using the following syntax:

```shell
terraform import katapult_object_storage_access_key.example objkey_aBcDeFg1234567
```

### What import gives you

Import is intended for **adopting an existing key into Terraform management**,
not for recovering credentials. After import you can:

* Rename the key (`name`).
* Change cluster-wide permissions (`all_buckets_read`, `all_objects_read`,
  `all_objects_write`).
* Destroy the key through Terraform.
* Reference its `id` from `read_key_ids` / `write_key_ids` on a bucket.

### What import does **not** give you

* `secret_access_key` will be empty and cannot be retrieved — the secret is
  only returned by the Katapult API at creation. If you need the secret for
  an existing key, either look it up in your secrets store (if you stashed
  it elsewhere) or replace the key with a freshly-created one.
* `access_key_id` and `server_url` are repopulated from the API on the next
  refresh, so they will be available — but on their own they are not enough
  to authenticate.
* Per-bucket associations (`read_buckets`, `write_buckets`) are not editable
  from this resource and are populated from the bucket side; if those need
  to change, update the corresponding
  [`katapult_object_storage_bucket`](./object_storage_bucket.md) resource.

If your goal is to rotate credentials, prefer creating a new key alongside
the old one, updating consumers to the new credentials, and then destroying
the old key (see [Credential Lifecycle](#credential-lifecycle)). Importing
the old key does not produce a usable secret to rotate from.

## Related Resources

* [`katapult_object_storage_account`](./object_storage_account.md) — the
  account this key authenticates against.
* [`katapult_object_storage_bucket`](./object_storage_bucket.md) — the bucket
  that an access key reads from or writes to.
